title: cardamine amara subsp. opizii
intro: Cardamine amara subsp. opizii, also known as bitter woodcress, is a subspecies of the bitter cress plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the mustard family. It is primarily found in the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of Cardamine amara subsp. opizii include its expectorant, diuretic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relieve respiratory issues, urinary problems, and skin irritations. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to provide relief from its associated health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and promoting digestive health.
